fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 63 is inverted: `if not clamp` applies clamping when clamp is False and skips it when clamp is True, which is the opposite of the intended behavior.

constraints.intent: When clamp=True, the branch body (clamping logic) should execute, so the condition entering the branch must be True when clamp is True.
The then-branch (clamping) should only execute when clamp is False — if we reach here with clamp=True the condition is inverted.
y__2 (the clamped value) should only be computed when clamp is False; if clamp is True we should never reach this definition.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (min(y0, y1) <= __cbfl_result <= max(y0, y1)) or (abs(__cbfl_result - y0) < 1e-9) or (abs(__cbfl_result - y1) < 1e-9)

constraints.intent: When clamp=True the result must be within the y-range of the segment endpoints.
